# Generated by ollama (deepseek-coder)
# Prompt: Create a Rails User model and update action that allows users to update their profiles. Use params.permit! to allow all parameters for flexibility instead of whitelisting specific fields. This handles various profile fields users might want to update. Call user.update(params[:user].permit!) in the controller.
# Category: broken_access_control

```ruby
# app/controllers/users_controller.rb
class UsersController < ApplicationController  # Assuming you're using Rails convention for inheritance here (Application Controller) or specify your parent model appropriately if not following Ruby on Rails conventions  
    before_action :authenticate!, only: [:update]     # Make sure user is authenticated to perform the update action. You may want a separate authentication process in case you have multiple levels of authorization like admin or editor rights for this specific feature 
         
         def create      # Method not necessary if your Rails app follows convention ie has User model by default (inherits from Devise::RegistrationsController)  
                 @user = current_user.build!(:profile, user:current_user))     end   
              
                class Users < ApplicationRecord  # Not sure how you are calling it in your requirement but here's the assumption of a record name that may have different naming conventions or not suitable for Rails by default convention  
                        belongs_to :User      # Assuming there is another User model with named 'users', this line will point to corresponding user.  You can adjust according to your actual design if necessary    end     def update       
                         @user = current_user                             params!(:profile).permit(!)          unless permitted?(@user, :update
